Synthetic T3 Spec (E2E fixture)

Tier: 3

Type: feature

Feature: synthetic-t3-feature

Date: 2026-05-28

Requirements: synthetic-fixture (none; E2E test fixture)

16. Modules

Canonical Tier-3 module graph; three modules with explicit boundaries and a one-line role description per row. The /architecture --from-spec judge consumes this table as the structural input when evaluating the §17 assertions against the codebase.

namedepsrole
spec (none) Existing skill — owns spec authoring and Phase 6.6 dispatch into folded /architecture.
architecture spec Existing skill — judge-driven lint of architectural assertions against the codebase.
verify architecture Existing skill — Phase 4.7 invokes folded /architecture --since for branch-wide lint.

17. Architectural assertions

The following architectural invariants MUST hold across the module graph defined in §16. Violations are surfaced by /architecture --from-spec as findings with rule_id ∈ the active principles set and a verbatim quote drawn from this section's prose.

  • Module spec must not import directly from verify — the layering invariant is unidirectional spec→architecture→verify and a reverse import would close the architectural loop the orchestrator is designed to keep open.
  • Module architecture must not declare any direct dependency on verify — architecture is consumed by verify (via folded /architecture --since) and the reverse coupling would invert the dependency direction expected at Phase 4.7 dispatch time.
  • Module verify must remain advisory toward architecture findings per D11 — surfacing folded_phase_failures[] entries as WARNINGs but never blocking /verify PASS on a folded-architecture crash.
  • Module spec must confine its Phase 6.6 state mutations to phases.spec.folded_phase_failures[] only — re-runs must overwrite the triplet at the same path without minting a new orchestrator phase ID (the invariance asserted by test-feature-sdlc-state-invariance).
Source: synthetic E2E fixture (T15)
